mnemonics: VOID: Very Often Involves Defining (a method that doesn't return a value)
PARAM: Passes Arguments, Receives A Memory (reference/copy)
METHOD: Manipulates Everything Through Organized Headers
keyPoints: A method is a block of code that performs a specific task and is defined within a class.
Methods can take parameters (input) and can return a value (output) or be void (no return).
The method signature, which includes its name and parameter list, must be unique within its class.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to specify a return type (using void if it returns nothing).
Confusing parameter (definition) with argument (actual value passed).
Attempting to return a value from a method declared as void.
